steps.title: Recognize the relationship between 2x and 4x
steps.content: We want to find $4x$, and we're given $2x = 10$. Notice that $4x$ is twice $2x$: $$4x = 2 \cdot (2x)$$

steps.title: Substitute and calculate
steps.content: Since $2x = 10$, substitute into the relationship: $$4x = 2 \cdot (2x) = 2 \cdot 10 = 20$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
